deep_dive.title: MeshLoom: Feed-Forward Non-Rigid Registration of Mesh Sequences
deep_dive.abstract: We present MeshLoom, a feed-forward registration network that directly reconstructs vertex deformations across mesh sequences. Our approach advances non-rigid registration beyond existing models, which are typically constrained by costly per-instance optimization, narrow object categories, pairwise-only inputs, or merely intermediate outputs. The network is simple and efficient, registering multiple meshes within seconds. At its core lies a topology-aware encoder--decoder design. Specifically, we first introduce a topology-aware point representation that encodes the anchor (reference) mesh's topology into its per-vertex features. This representation strengthens the network's understanding of the anchor-mesh geometry and disambiguates points that are Euclidean-close yet geodesically distant. We then propose a multi-modal encoder that fuses this anchor-mesh representation with complementary cues from each frame, such as shape latents and image features. These multi-source signals are compressed into a compact global motion embedding that captures dense inter-frame correspondence. A lightweight decoder then queries this global embedding with the anchor-mesh point representation, retrieving per-vertex deformations at target timestamps. Through extensive experiments across diverse motions and object categories, we show that MeshLoom achieves state-of-the-art results on non-rigid registration. In addition, we find that our global embedding-then-query paradigm naturally enables the network to generate deformations at intermediate timestamps, which extends MeshLoom to motion interpolation and mesh morphing. Project page: https://meshloom.github.io/ .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward non-rigid registration vs optimization-based; efficient and direct
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 前馈式非刚性配准与基于优化的方法对比，效率高且直接
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 前馈式非刚性配准与基于优化的方法对比，效率高且直接
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 前馈式非刚性配准与基于优化的方法对比，效率高且直接
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MeshLoom presents a feed-forward network for non-rigid registration of mesh sequences, directly regressing vertex deformations without per-instance optimization. It achieves accurate registration in seconds, outperforming optimization-based methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有非刚性配准方法通常依赖耗时的逐实例优化，且局限于特定类别。MeshLoom提出一种拓扑感知的前馈配准网络，直接从参考网格回归顶点变形，无需优化。该网络在数秒内完成多网格配准，在多个基准上精度优于优化方法，展示了前馈策略在3D配准中的潜力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有非刚性配准方法通常依赖耗时的逐实例优化，且局限于特定类别。MeshLoom提出一种拓扑感知的前馈配准网络，直接从参考网格回归顶点变形，无需优化。该网络在数秒内完成多网格配准，在多个基准上精度优于优化方法，展示了前馈策略在3D配准中的潜力。

deep_dive.title: RegimeVGGT: Layer-Wise Spatially Preserving Redundancy Removal for Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer
deep_dive.abstract: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer (VGGT) recovers dense 3D scene structure from multi-view images in one forward pass, but quadratic cross-frame attention limits its scalability. Existing training-free accelerators reduce computation uniformly along one axis, missing layer heterogeneity. Our spectral, probing, and causal analyses reveal three regimes: shallow layers lack cross-view structure, middle layers drive cross-view alignment, and deep layers are redundant for dense geometry yet their cross-frame attention remains essential for pose. RegimeVGGT applies layer-wise U-shaped compression along two axes: Saliency-Guided Banded Merging protects geometry- and edge-salient tokens, while Selectively Protected K/V Downsampling preserves cross-frame spatial coverage and the pose-critical path through a phase-shifted spatial grid, a reference-frame anchor, and uncompressed camera/register tokens. Training-free, RegimeVGGT achieves a 6.7x speedup over VGGT* at matched reconstruction quality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward 3D scene reconstruction acceleration for VGGT
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RegimeVGGT accelerates VGGT's feed-forward 3D reconstruction by layer-wise redundancy removal, preserving geometry and pose accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对VGGT前馈3D重建中二次交叉帧注意力导致的扩展性瓶颈，通过分析不同层的特性，提出分层U型压缩策略，在保持几何和位姿精度的同时大幅减少计算量，实验证明该方法在加速比和重建质量之间取得良好平衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对VGGT前馈3D重建中二次交叉帧注意力导致的扩展性瓶颈，通过分析不同层的特性，提出分层U型压缩策略，在保持几何和位姿精度的同时大幅减少计算量，实验证明该方法在加速比和重建质量之间取得良好平衡。

deep_dive.title: Hand-4DGS: Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting for 4D Hand Reconstruction from Egocentric Videos
deep_dive.abstract: Dynamic 3D hand reconstruction from egocentric videos is essential for next-generation computing platforms such as AR/VR and AI glasses. Despite its importance, most prior works focus either on multi-view 3D hand reconstruction or on 4D human body reconstruction. Egocentric 4D hand reconstruction remains challenging due to fast head motion, rapid hand dynamics, severe occlusions, and inherent ambiguity from single-view observations. To address these challenges, we introduce Hand-4DGS, the first feed-forward framework for reconstructing dynamic 4D hands directly from egocentric videos, enabling both fast (~60 FPS) inference and strong generalization. Our approach incorporates a mesh-guided representation for structural priors and temporal convolutions to model dynamic motion. We evaluate our framework on two challenging egocentric datasets, H2O and ARCTIC, and demonstrate significant improvements over baselines. Our method benefits from the generalization capability of feed-forward networks and effective 2D image supervision through Gaussian splatting, without requiring expensive 3D hand pose ground-truth annotations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward framework for dynamic 3D hand reconstruction from egocentric video
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于第一人称视频中动态3D手部重建的前馈框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于第一人称视频中动态3D手部重建的前馈框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于第一人称视频中动态3D手部重建的前馈框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Hand-4DGS is a feed-forward framework using 3D Gaussian splatting for real-time 4D hand reconstruction from egocentric videos, achieving ~60 FPS inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Hand-4DGS，首个从第一人称视频重建动态4D手部的前馈框架，结合3D高斯泼溅和网格引导表示，实现快速推理（约60FPS）和强泛化能力。针对头部快速运动、手部动态和严重遮挡等挑战，该方法无需优化即可直接输出手部几何。实验证明其在多个数据集上优于现有优化方法，为AR/VR等应用提供了高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出Hand-4DGS，首个从第一人称视频重建动态4D手部的前馈框架，结合3D高斯泼溅和网格引导表示，实现快速推理（约60FPS）和强泛化能力。针对头部快速运动、手部动态和严重遮挡等挑战，该方法无需优化即可直接输出手部几何。实验证明其在多个数据集上优于现有优化方法，为AR/VR等应用提供了高效方案。

deep_dive.title: MoVerse: Real-Time Video World Modeling with Panoramic Gaussian Scaffold
deep_dive.abstract: We present MoVerse, a real-time video world model that creates an interactively navigable scene from a single narrow-field-of-view image. This setting is challenging because the input observes only a small fraction of the environment, while interactive roaming requires a complete surrounding world, persistent geometry, controllable camera motion, and temporally coherent high-fidelity observations. MoVerse addresses this problem by separating world construction from observation rendering. It first expands the input into a gravity-aligned 360$^\circ$ panorama with topology-aware diffusion, closing the missing field of view before 3D reasoning. It then lifts the panorama into a persistent 3D Gaussian scaffold using panoramic geometry-aware residual prediction, yielding a dense and directly renderable spatial memory. Finally, a Gaussian-conditioned video renderer translates scaffold renderings along user-specified camera trajectories into photorealistic video. To make this renderer practical for interaction, we train a bidirectional diffusion teacher for high-quality conditional rendering and distill it into a causal autoregressive student for bounded-latency streaming. This design combines the controllability and long-range consistency of explicit 3D representations with the perceptual quality of generative video models. MoVerse supports real-time scene roaming at 8~FPS on a single NVIDIA RTX~4090 GPU, demonstrating a practical path toward single-image world creation with interactive video output.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: single image to navigable 3D scene generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 单张图像生成可导航3D场景
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 单张图像生成可导航3D场景
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 单张图像生成可导航3D场景
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MoVerse generates an interactively navigable 3D scene from a single narrow-FoV image by first expanding to a 360 panorama then lifting to a Gaussian scaffold, enabling real-time world modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法从单张窄视场图像生成可交互漫游的3D场景面临挑战。MoVerse提出将世界构建与观测渲染分离，首先通过拓扑感知扩散将输入扩展为重力对齐的360度全景，再使用全景几何感知残差预测将全景提升为持久3D高斯脚手架。该方法在保持实时性的同时，实现了从任意视角的高保真观测，为视频世界模型提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有方法从单张窄视场图像生成可交互漫游的3D场景面临挑战。MoVerse提出将世界构建与观测渲染分离，首先通过拓扑感知扩散将输入扩展为重力对齐的360度全景，再使用全景几何感知残差预测将全景提升为持久3D高斯脚手架。该方法在保持实时性的同时，实现了从任意视角的高保真观测，为视频世界模型提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: VideoWeave: Unlocking Geometric Consistency in Video Generation via Joint Geometry-Video Modeling
deep_dive.abstract: Large-scale video diffusion models often fail to preserve 3D structure over time, causing geometric drift and implausible motion under viewpoint changes. Existing methods usually enforce geometric consistency by using explicit geometry reconstructions, such as depth maps, point clouds, or reconstructed 3D structures, to define conditions, supervision, or reward signals, making the generator sensitive to errors from upstream geometry pipelines. We propose VideoWeave, a latent-space post-training framework that uses implicit geometry-model features to constrain the generative distribution, providing a more flexible and non-rigid form of guidance that mitigates the impact of reconstruction errors from geometry models. Specifically, VideoWeave adapts these features into geometry latents and jointly models them with video latents in a shared denoising space, allowing geometry to shape the generative distribution during training. To support this process, we build GeoVid-80K, an 80K-video dataset with paired appearance and geometry representations. Experiments on text-to-video and image-to-video generation show that VideoWeave improves geometric coherence while preserving strong visual quality. VideoWeave project page at https://videoweave.github.io/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Video generation with geometric consistency using implicit geometry features
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用隐式几何特征实现视频生成的几何一致性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用隐式几何特征实现视频生成的几何一致性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用隐式几何特征实现视频生成的几何一致性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VideoWeave uses implicit geometry features in a latent-space post-training framework to improve geometric consistency in video diffusion models without explicit reconstruction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视频扩散模型在视角变化下容易出现几何漂移。现有方法依赖显式几何重建（如深度图、点云）作为条件，但受上游误差影响。VideoWeave提出一种隐式几何特征引导的潜在空间后训练框架，约束生成分布以避免几何不一致。实验表明该方法有效缓解几何漂移，生成更合理的运动。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 视频扩散模型在视角变化下容易出现几何漂移。现有方法依赖显式几何重建（如深度图、点云）作为条件，但受上游误差影响。VideoWeave提出一种隐式几何特征引导的潜在空间后训练框架，约束生成分布以避免几何不一致。实验表明该方法有效缓解几何漂移，生成更合理的运动。

deep_dive.title: Intrinsic 4D Gaussian Segmentation from Scene Cues
deep_dive.abstract: Dynamic 4D Gaussian Splatting reconstructs deforming scenes with high fidelity and is increasingly adopted as a representation for dynamic 3D scenes. Putting such a scene to use, for editing, manipulation or motion analysis, first requires segmenting it: grouping the Gaussian primitives into coherent objects. Current pipelines obtain this grouping by importing 2D masks from foundation models such as SAM and lifting or distilling them into the Gaussian representation. In dynamic scenes these masks must be generated across many frames and views, which is costly, and the resulting segmentation can depend strongly on the quality and consistency of those external masks. We ask how much object-level structure can instead be recovered from the Gaussians themselves, and propose Intrinsic-GS, a training-free, mask-free method that builds a sparse affinity graph over Gaussian primitives from appearance, orientation, scale, deformation-trajectory and non-learned rendered-boundary cues. The graph is partitioned with Leiden community detection, requiring no foundation model and no learned feature field. On the standard 4D Gaussian segmentation benchmarks, Neu3D and HyperNeRF, Intrinsic-GS recovers substantial object structure without mask supervision, reaching 0.746 mIoU on Neu3D and 0.575 on HyperNeRF; on Neu3D, a geometry-only variant reaches 0.902 mIoU, matching SAM-supervised TRASE. On HyperNeRF, Intrinsic-GS runs 12.5x faster than the mask-generation and feature-rendering stages used by mask-supervised pipelines. These results suggest that much of the segmentation signal is already encoded in the Gaussians themselves, offering a fast, mask-free direction for 3D and 4D Gaussian segmentation that may also point toward more generalizable, robust segmentation in settings where external masks are unreliable or expensive.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: segmenting Gaussian primitives for 4D dynamic scenes without external masks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需外部掩码即可分割4D动态场景中的高斯基元
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需外部掩码即可分割4D动态场景中的高斯基元
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无需外部掩码即可分割4D动态场景中的高斯基元
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a method to segment dynamic 4D Gaussian splatting scenes into objects using intrinsic cues from the Gaussians themselves, avoiding costly 2D mask generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对动态4D高斯泼溅场景中现有分割方法依赖多视角2D掩码导致成本高且不一致的问题，本文提出仅利用高斯基元内在的结构线索（如位置、颜色、运动）进行物体级分割，无需任何外部掩码。实验表明，该方法能有效恢复物体级别的结构，在分割质量和效率上均优于依赖外部模型的基线方法。该工作展示了高斯表示本身蕴含丰富的语义结构，为动态场景的高效编辑与操作提供了新的途径。

deep_dive.title: A Cross-Model VLM-Judge Protocol for Single-Image 3D Mesh Quality (and Why Cheap Proxies Fall Short)
deep_dive.abstract: Single-image-to-3D generators are improving quickly, but there is no agreed, human-free way to tell whether one generated mesh is better than another. Practitioners commonly rely on cheap automatic proxies (render-space CLIP similarity and mesh geometry-validity statistics), yet how well these track perceived quality is unestablished. We make two contributions. First, we propose and validate a reproducible VLM-judge evaluation protocol: a fixed 24-view headless render rig, two independent vision-language judge families, and a mandatory position-bias correction that queries both presentation orders and keeps only order-consistent verdicts. The two judge families agree substantially with each other (Cohen's kappa = 0.66), well above the chance-agreement floor. Second, using this protocol as the reference, we show the cheap proxies do not substitute for it. Geometry validity is only a weak signal on average (because, as we show, it is bimodal) and stays below our pre-registered target, while render-CLIP is at chance. A learned Bradley-Terry head collapses onto a single manifoldness statistic (giving render-CLIP a negative weight) and matches geometry-only exactly, so learning the feature weights buys nothing. The proxy is also bimodal: it is significantly above chance on contrasts with visible geometric defects but at chance on ambiguous contrasts, consistent with geometry validity tracking the judge only when the defect is visually salient. We therefore recommend the VLM-judge protocol as a reliable, reproducible evaluator under the conditions tested (two feed-forward generators on Google Scanned Objects, with a face-drop degradation regime) and advise against geometry/CLIP proxies as optimization targets.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估单图像到3D网格的质量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估单图像到3D网格的质量

quick_skim.title: Edit3DGS: Unified Framework for Dynamic Head Editing via 2D Instruction-Guided Diffusion and 3D Gaussian Splatting
quick_skim.abstract: We present Edit3DGS, a unified framework for dynamic 3D head editing that integrates 2D instruction-guided diffusion with 3D Gaussian splatting. Unlike prior approaches that separately address frame-based edits or static 3D reconstruction, our method couples semantic controllability in the image domain with photorealistic, temporally consistent 3D representations. Given an input video, editable facial regions are masked and modified using a text-conditioned diffusion model to support fine-grained operations such as expression transformation, attribute modification, and appearance refinement. The edited frames are then aggregated through 3D Gaussian splatting to produce a coherent, high-fidelity avatar that preserves both identity and motion dynamics. To enforce consistency, Edit3DGS incorporates multi-view batch editing and lightweight inpainting strategies that recover lost expressions across timesteps. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework enables controllable, artifact-free head editing with smooth temporal transitions, offering practical applications in virtual avatars, immersive communication, film production, and interactive media.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: text-conditioned diffusion for dynamic 3D head editing with 3D Gaussian splatting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 文本条件扩散用于动态3D头部编辑结合3D高斯泼溅

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 文本条件扩散用于动态3D头部编辑结合3D高斯泼溅
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Edit3DGS unifies 2D instruction-guided diffusion and 3D Gaussian splatting for photorealistic, temporally consistent dynamic 3D head editing from video input.

quick_skim.title: Splaxel: Efficient Distributed Training of 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-scale Scene Reconstruction via Pixel-level Communication
quick_skim.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enables high-fidelity and real-time 3D scene reconstruction, but scaling training to large-scale scenes requires optimizing hundreds of millions of Gaussians across multiple GPUs. Existing distributed approaches either partition scenes into isolated regions, causing global inconsistency, or rely on global Gaussian-level exchanges, which lead to substantial growth in inter-GPU communication and quickly dominate iteration time.   We propose Splaxel, a communication-efficient distributed 3DGS training framework based on pixel-level local rendering and global composition. Instead of synchronizing Gaussians, each GPU renders its local subset and exchanges only partial pixel values, maintaining mathematical consistency while keeping communication cost stable as the scene size increases. Splaxel further reduces pixel-level redundancy through geometric and transmittance visibility prediction and improves GPU utilization via conflict-free camera-view consolidation. Evaluated on large-scale datasets with up to 120M Gaussians, Splaxel achieves up to 7.6$\times$ speedup over the state-of-the-art distributed 3DGS framework while preserving high reconstruction quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes efficient distributed training for large-scale 3DGS reconstruction, addressing scalability of 3D reconstruction models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出高效分布式训练方法用于大规模3DGS场景重建，解决3D重建模型的可扩展性问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出高效分布式训练方法用于大规模3DGS场景重建，解决3D重建模型的可扩展性问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出高效分布式训练方法用于大规模3DGS场景重建，解决3D重建模型的可扩展性问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Splaxel introduces a communication-efficient distributed 3DGS training framework using pixel-level local rendering and global composition, enabling large-scale scene reconstruction with hundreds of millions of Gaussians across GPUs while minimizing inter-GPU communication overhead.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大规模3D高斯泼溅训练中GPU间通信瓶颈问题，Splaxel提出基于像素级局部渲染与全局合成的分布式框架，每个GPU渲染局部子集并仅交换部分像素，显著降低通信量，实现高效大规模场景重建。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大规模3D高斯泼溅训练中GPU间通信瓶颈问题，Splaxel提出基于像素级局部渲染与全局合成的分布式框架，每个GPU渲染局部子集并仅交换部分像素，显著降低通信量，实现高效大规模场景重建。

quick_skim.title: CAOA -- Completion-Assisted Object-CAD Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Accurately aligning CAD models to their corresponding objects in indoor RGB-D scans is a central challenge in 3D semantic reconstruction. The task requires estimating a 9-Degree-of-Freedom (DoF) pose-position, rotation, and scale along three axes-but is hindered by noisy and incomplete scans, as well as segmentation errors that cause geometric distortions. We present Completion-Assisted Object-CAD Alignment (CAOA), a method that integrates a semantically and contextually aware point cloud completion module with a symmetry-aware relative pose estimation algorithm, enabling precise alignment of CAD models to scanned objects. Existing completion methods are typically trained and evaluated on synthetic datasets, which often fail to generalize to real-world scans. To bridge this gap, we introduce a synthetic data generation strategy tailored to indoor scenes, significantly reducing the synthetic-to-real domain gap-validated through quantitative comparisons with widely used completion datasets. In addition, we release S2C-Completion, an expert-annotated dataset of over 8,500 object-CAD pairs from Scan2CAD, created for real-world indoor single-object completion and intended as a new benchmark for this task. For object-CAD alignment, we incorporate symmetry information via a symmetry-aware loss, improving robustness to symmetric ambiguities. On the Scan2CAD benchmark, CAOA achieves a 17% accuracy improvement over state-of-the-art methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: point cloud completion for CAD alignment in 3D reconstruction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 点云补全用于3D重建中的CAD对齐
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 点云补全用于3D重建中的CAD对齐
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 点云补全用于3D重建中的CAD对齐
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CAOA combines a semantic point cloud completion module with symmetry-aware pose estimation for accurate CAD model alignment to scanned objects, improving 3D semantic reconstruction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对3D语义重建中CAD模型与扫描对象对齐困难的问题，CAOA引入了语义和上下文感知的点云补全模块，结合对称性感知的相对位姿估计算法，实现了精确的9自由度对齐。方法在真实扫描上表现优异，有效缓解了扫描噪声和不完整性带来的挑战。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对3D语义重建中CAD模型与扫描对象对齐困难的问题，CAOA引入了语义和上下文感知的点云补全模块，结合对称性感知的相对位姿估计算法，实现了精确的9自由度对齐。方法在真实扫描上表现优异，有效缓解了扫描噪声和不完整性带来的挑战。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to feed forward 3D重建, especially work that connects or combines: feed-forward neural networks for 3D reconstruction; direct mapping from pixels to 3D geometry using feed-forward networks; state of the art feed forward 3D reconstruction methods; feed forward vs optimization based 3D reconstruction; efficient large scale 3D reconstruction models.

quick_skim.title: NeuMesh++: Towards Versatile and Efficient Volumetric Editing with Disentangled Neural Mesh-based Implicit Field
quick_skim.abstract: Recently neural implicit rendering techniques have evolved rapidly and demonstrated significant advantages in novel view synthesis and 3D scene reconstruction. However, existing neural rendering methods for editing purposes offer limited functionalities, e.g., rigid transformation and category-specific editing. In this paper, we present a novel mesh-based representation by encoding the neural radiance field with disentangled geometry, texture, and semantic codes on mesh vertices, which empowers a set of efficient and comprehensive editing functionalities, including mesh-guided geometry editing, designated texture editing with texture swapping, filling and painting operations, and semantic-guided editing. To this end, we develop several techniques including a novel local space parameterization to enhance rendering quality and training stability, a learnable modification color on vertex to improve the fidelity of texture editing, a spatial-aware optimization strategy to realize precise texture editing, and a semantic-aided region selection to ease the laborious annotation of implicit field editing. Extensive experiments and editing examples on both real and synthetic datasets demonstrate the superiority of our method on representation quality and editing ability. Project page: https://zju3dv.github.io/neumeshplusplus/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural mesh-based implicit field for volumetric editing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于神经网格隐式场的体编辑
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于神经网格隐式场的体编辑
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于神经网格隐式场的体编辑
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: NeuMesh++ encodes geometry, texture, and semantics on mesh vertices for versatile volumetric editing including geometry and texture manipulation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有神经渲染编辑方法功能有限，该论文提出NeuMesh++，在网格顶点上编码解耦的几何、纹理和语义码，支持网格引导的几何编辑、纹理交换填充和语义编辑。该方法实现了高效且多功能的体编辑，扩展了神经渲染在3D内容创建中的应用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有神经渲染编辑方法功能有限，该论文提出NeuMesh++，在网格顶点上编码解耦的几何、纹理和语义码，支持网格引导的几何编辑、纹理交换填充和语义编辑。该方法实现了高效且多功能的体编辑，扩展了神经渲染在3D内容创建中的应用。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 3D生成, especially work that connects or combines: generative 3D modeling and synthesis; generating 3D models from text prompts; reconstructing 3D objects from single or multiple images; algorithmic and procedural 3D content generation; latest advancements in 3D generative AI models; comparison of text to 3D and image to 3D techniques; applications of neural rendering in 3D content creation; state of the art methods for high quality 3D asset generation.

quick_skim.title: FlowObject: Flow Steering for Bridging Generative Priors and Reconstruction Fidelity
quick_skim.abstract: Recovering complete 3D representations of objects from few casual image captures remains a significant challenge. Recent 3D generative models, particularly those based on Flow-Matching (FM), can synthesize high-quality textured assets; however, they often suffer from ''synthetic bias'' where learned priors override observational evidence, alongside a lack of alignment with the observed instance. Conversely, optimization-based methods like 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) provide high fidelity on visible surfaces but fail to reason about unobserved geometry. In this paper, we present FlowObject, a framework that reformulates sparse-view 3D reconstruction as a training-free, guided inverse problem. Our approach applies a dual-space guidance strategy to steer the Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE) trajectory of a flow-matching model, enabling the completion of unseen regions through learned generative priors while enforcing strict consistency with real-world observations. By integrating a 3DGS refinement stage, FlowObject further bridges the gap between ''synthetic-looking'' generative outputs and photorealistic reconstructions. Comprehensive benchmarks on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that current state-of-the-art methods often struggle to achieve geometric completeness and observational consistency simultaneously, especially under severe occlusions. In contrast, our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art generative models and optimization-based frameworks in both geometric completeness and view-dependent appearance fidelity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: compares generative priors (feed-forward flow-matching) versus optimization-based 3DGS for sparse-view reconstruction, aligning with feed-forward vs optimization debate
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 比较了生成先验（前馈流匹配）和基于优化的3DGS在稀疏视图重建中的表现，契合前馈与优化对比需求
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 比较了生成先验（前馈流匹配）和基于优化的3DGS在稀疏视图重建中的表现，契合前馈与优化对比需求
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 比较了生成先验（前馈流匹配）和基于优化的3DGS在稀疏视图重建中的表现，契合前馈与优化对比需求
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FlowObject reformulates sparse-view 3D reconstruction as a training-free guided inverse problem, bridging generative Flow-Matching priors with optimization-based 3DGS fidelity, achieving high-quality complete 3D objects from few images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对少视图3D重建中生成模型存在合成偏差、优化方法无法推理未观测区域的问题，FlowObject提出无需训练的引导逆问题框架，结合流匹配生成先验与3DGS优化保真度，在保持可观测区域高保真的同时补全不可见几何，为前馈与优化方法融合提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对少视图3D重建中生成模型存在合成偏差、优化方法无法推理未观测区域的问题，FlowObject提出无需训练的引导逆问题框架，结合流匹配生成先验与3DGS优化保真度，在保持可观测区域高保真的同时补全不可见几何，为前馈与优化方法融合提供了新思路。
